audit_items.proof_trace: The IETF C LDAP API spec (confirmed in the codebase) explicitly states ldapai_vendor_name 'may be set to NULL if no name is available'; in options.c:171, LDAP_STRDUP can return NULL on OOM and that failure is silently swallowed (rc stays LDAP_OPT_SUCCESS), so ldap_get_option returns success with a NULL vendor_name. apitest.c line 144 passes api.ldapai_vendor_name to printf("%s") without any NULL guard, which is undefined behaviour per C standard — inconsistent with how ldapai_extensions is guarded at line 102.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A low-memory environment where LDAP_STRDUP fails for LDAP_VENDOR_NAME causes ldap_get_option to silently return NULL in ldapai_vendor_name; the subsequent printf at line 144 invokes undefined behaviour that, on C library implementations that dereference the pointer, produces a crash.
